words.definitionEn: an attitude towards something or an opinion that you make clear to people
words.exampleEn: He has avoided taking a firm stand.

words.definitionEn: existence
words.exampleEn: The Irish Free State came into being in 1922.

words.definitionEn: to look at somebody/something for a long time
words.exampleEn: I screamed and everyone stared.

words.definitionEn: to suffer or die because you do not have enough food to eat; to make somebody suffer or die in this way
words.exampleEn: The animals were left to starve to death.

words.definitionEn: the legal position of a person, group or country
words.exampleEn: They were granted refugee status.

words.definitionEn: gradually and in an even and regular way
words.exampleEn: The company's exports have been increasing steadily.

words.definitionEn: developing, growing, etc. gradually and in an even and regular way
words.exampleEn: We've had five years of steady economic growth.

words.definitionEn: the hot gas that water changes into when it boils
words.exampleEn: Steam rose from the boiling kettle.

words.definitionEn: a strong, hard metal that is made of a mixture of iron and carbon
words.exampleEn: the iron and steel industry

words.definitionEn: rising or falling quickly, not gradually
words.exampleEn: a steep hill/slope

words.definitionEn: to lift your foot and move it in a particular direction or put it on or in something; to move a short distance
words.exampleEn: to step onto/off a bus

words.definitionEn: made of or covered in a substance that sticks to things that touch it
words.exampleEn: sticky fingers covered in jam

words.definitionEn: difficult to bend or move
words.exampleEn: a sheet of stiff black cardboard

words.definitionEn: to make something develop or become more active; to encourage something
words.exampleEn: The exhibition has stimulated interest in her work.

words.definitionEn: a supply of goods that is available for sale in a shop
words.exampleEn: We have a fast turnover of stock.

words.definitionEn: a small, narrow river
words.exampleEn: a mountain stream

words.definitionEn: to become more powerful or effective; to make somebody/something more powerful or effective
words.exampleEn: Her position in the party has strengthened in recent weeks.

words.definitionEn: an area of land or water, especially a long one
words.exampleEn: a particularly dangerous stretch of road

words.definitionEn: to make something longer, wider or looser, for example by pulling it; to become longer, etc. in this way
words.exampleEn: Is there any way of stretching shoes?

words.definitionEn: that must be obeyed exactly
words.exampleEn: strict rules/regulations

words.definitionEn: with a lot of control and rules that must be obeyed
words.exampleEn: She was brought up very strictly.

words.definitionEn: a period of time when an organized group of employees of a company stops working because of an argument over pay or conditions
words.exampleEn: the miners'/firefighters'/teachers' strike

words.definitionEn: to hit somebody/something hard or with force
words.exampleEn: strike somebody/something, The ship struck a rock.

words.definitionEn: an act of hitting a ball, for example with a bat or racket
words.exampleEn: What a beautiful stroke!

words.definitionEn: to arrange or organize something into a system or pattern
words.exampleEn: structure something, How well does the teacher structure the lessons?

words.definitionEn: a hard fight in which people try to obtain or achieve something, especially something that somebody else does not want them to have
words.exampleEn: Marx wrote about the class struggle.

words.definitionEn: to try very hard to do something when it is difficult or when there are a lot of problems
words.exampleEn: life as a struggling artist (= one who is very poor)

words.definitionEn: in a way that is correct and true in every detail
words.exampleEn: The article accurately reflects public opinion.

words.definitionEn: to fill a space or container tightly with something
words.exampleEn: stuff A with B, She had 500 envelopes to stuff with leaflets.

words.definitionEn: extremely attractive or impressive
words.exampleEn: You look absolutely stunning!

words.definitionEn: likely to be affected by something, especially something bad
words.exampleEn: At this stage these are proposals and are still subject to change.

words.definitionEn: to give a document, proposal, etc. to somebody in authority so that they can study or consider it
words.exampleEn: submit something, to submit an application/a claim/a proposal

words.definitionEn: happening or coming after something else
words.exampleEn: subsequent generations

words.definitionEn: afterwards; later; after something else has happened
words.exampleEn: The original interview notes were subsequently lost.

words.definitionEn: an area where people live that is outside the centre of a city
words.exampleEn: a suburb of London

words.definitionEn: physical or mental pain
words.exampleEn: Death finally brought an end to her suffering.

words.definitionEn: enough for a particular purpose; as much as you need
words.exampleEn: Allow sufficient time to get there.

words.definitionEn: enough for a particular purpose; as much as you need
words.exampleEn: The following day she felt sufficiently well to go to work.

words.definitionEn: improving a situation; having a helpful or useful effect
words.exampleEn: A good diet is beneficial to health.

words.definitionEn: an amount of money
words.exampleEn: You will be fined the sum of £200.

words.definitionEn: extremely good
words.exampleEn: a super meal

words.definitionEn: a doctor who is trained to perform surgery (= medical operations that involve cutting open a person’s body)
words.exampleEn: a brain/heart surgeon

words.definitionEn: medical treatment of injuries or diseases that involves cutting open a person’s body and often removing or replacing some parts; the branch of medicine connected with this treatment
words.exampleEn: major/minor surgery

words.definitionEn: not straight
words.exampleEn: a piece of bent wire

words.definitionEn: to be all around something/somebody
words.exampleEn: surround something/somebody, Tall trees surround the lake.

words.definitionEn: that is near or around something
words.exampleEn: Oxford and the surrounding area

words.definitionEn: to look carefully at the whole of something, especially in order to get a general impression of it
words.exampleEn: The next morning we surveyed the damage caused by the fire.

words.definitionEn: the state of continuing to live or exist, often despite difficulty or danger
words.exampleEn: the struggle/battle/fight for survival

words.definitionEn: next to or at the side of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: He sat beside her all night.

words.definitionEn: a person who continues to live, especially despite being nearly killed or experiencing great danger or difficulty
words.exampleEn: the sole/only survivor of the massacre

words.definitionEn: a person who is suspected of a crime or of having done something wrong
words.exampleEn: a murder suspect

words.definitionEn: to have an idea that something is probably true or likely to happen, especially something bad, but without having definite proof
words.exampleEn: As I had suspected all along, he was not a real policeman.

words.definitionEn: to hang something from something else
words.exampleEn: be suspended from something, A lamp was suspended from the ceiling.

words.definitionEn: involving the use of natural products and energy in a way that does not harm the environment
words.exampleEn: sustainable forest management

words.definitionEn: to make food, drink, etc. go down your throat into your stomach
words.exampleEn: swallow (something), Always chew food well before swallowing it.

words.definitionEn: used for making an extra comment that adds to what you have just said
words.exampleEn: I don't really want to go. Besides, it's too late now.

words.definitionEn: to use rude or offensive language, usually because you are angry
words.exampleEn: She fell over and swore loudly.

words.definitionEn: to clean a room, surface, etc. using a broom (= a type of brush on a long handle)
words.exampleEn: sweep (something), to sweep the floor

words.definitionEn: in addition to somebody/something; apart from somebody/something
words.exampleEn: We have lots of things in common besides music.

words.definitionEn: a small device that you press or move up and down in order to turn a light or piece of electrical equipment on and off
words.exampleEn: a light switch

words.definitionEn: kind to somebody who is hurt or sad; showing that you understand and care about their problems
words.exampleEn: a sympathetic listener

words.definitionEn: the feeling of being sorry for somebody; showing that you understand and care about somebody’s problems
words.exampleEn: sympathy for somebody, to express/feel sympathy for somebody

words.definitionEn: to make a determined effort to deal with a difficult problem or situation
words.exampleEn: The government is determined to tackle inflation.

words.definitionEn: a small piece of paper, cloth, plastic, etc. attached to something to identify it or give information about it
words.exampleEn: He put name tags on all his shirts.

words.definitionEn: to fasten a tag onto something/somebody
words.exampleEn: Each animal was tagged with a number for identification.

words.definitionEn: a story created using the imagination, especially one that is full of action and adventure
words.exampleEn: a coming-of-age/morality tale

words.definitionEn: a large container for holding liquid or gas
words.exampleEn: a fuel/water/storage tank

words.definitionEn: a device for controlling the flow of water from a pipe into a bath or sink
words.exampleEn: bath taps

words.definitionEn: to hit somebody/something quickly and lightly
words.exampleEn: tap (away) (at something), Someone tapped at the door.

words.definitionEn: to aim an attack or a criticism at somebody/something
words.exampleEn: target somebody/something, He accused the group of deliberately targeting civilians.

words.definitionEn: an arrangement to risk money, etc. on the result of a particular event; the money that you risk in this way
words.exampleEn: to win/lose a bet

words.definitionEn: a hole that has been made in something by tearing
words.exampleEn: This sheet has a tear in it.

words.definitionEn: to damage something by pulling it apart or into pieces or by cutting it on something sharp; to become damaged in this way
words.exampleEn: tear something + adv./prep., I tore my jeans on the fence.

words.definitionEn: to risk money on a race or an event by trying to predict the result
words.exampleEn: You have to be over 16 to bet.

words.definitionEn: connected with technology
words.exampleEn: technological advances

words.definitionEn: the years of a person’s life when they are between 13 and 19 years old
words.exampleEn: in your teens, She began writing poetry in her teens.

words.definitionEn: a building used for religious worship, especially in religions other than Christianity
words.exampleEn: the Temple of Diana at Ephesus

words.definitionEn: in a way that lasts or is intended to last or be used only for a short time; in a way that is not permanent
words.exampleEn: We regret this service is temporarily unavailable.

words.definitionEn: lasting or intended to last or be used only for a short time; not permanent
words.exampleEn: to hire temporary workers

words.definitionEn: if somebody/something has a particular tendency, they are likely to behave or act in a particular way
words.exampleEn: to display artistic tendencies

words.definitionEn: a situation in which people do not trust each other, or feel unfriendly towards each other, and that may cause them to attack each other
words.exampleEn: There is mounting tension along the border.

words.definitionEn: to use a particular name or word to describe somebody/something
words.exampleEn: term somebody/something + adj., At his age, he can hardly be termed young.

words.definitionEn: a building or set of buildings at an airport where passengers arrive and leave
words.exampleEn: A second terminal was opened last year.

words.definitionEn: the conditions that people offer, demand or accept when they make an agreement, an arrangement or a contract
words.exampleEn: peace terms

words.definitionEn: to say that somebody has done something wrong or is guilty of something
words.exampleEn: accuse somebody of something, to accuse somebody of murder/a crime

words.definitionEn: very
words.exampleEn: I'm terribly sorry—did I hurt you?

words.definitionEn: to make somebody feel extremely frightened
words.exampleEn: terrify somebody, Flying terrifies her.

words.definitionEn: land that is under the control of a particular country or political leader
words.exampleEn: enemy/disputed/foreign territory

words.definitionEn: a feeling of extreme fear
words.exampleEn: a feeling of sheer/pure terror

words.definitionEn: the use of violent action in order to achieve political aims or to force a government to act
words.exampleEn: an act of terrorism

words.definitionEn: a person who takes part in terrorism
words.exampleEn: The terrorists are threatening to blow up the plane.

words.definitionEn: the activity of testing somebody/something in order to find something out, see if it works, etc.
words.exampleEn: nuclear testing

words.definitionEn: a book that teaches a particular subject and that is used especially in schools and colleges
words.exampleEn: a school/medical/history textbook

words.definitionEn: the crime of stealing something from a person or place
words.exampleEn: car theft

words.definitionEn: on the other side; further on
words.exampleEn: Snowdon and the mountains beyond were covered in snow.

words.definitionEn: a specialist who treats a particular type of illness or problem, or who uses a particular type of treatment
words.exampleEn: a speech therapist

words.definitionEn: the treatment of a physical problem or an illness
words.exampleEn: He is receiving therapy for cancer.

words.definitionEn: a long piece of writing completed by a student as part of a university degree, based on their own research
words.exampleEn: Students must submit a thesis on an agreed subject within four years.

words.definitionEn: on or to the further side of something
words.exampleEn: The road continues beyond the village up into the hills.

words.definitionEn: a strong feeling in favour of or against one group of people, or one side in an argument, often not based on fair judgement
words.exampleEn: accusations of political bias in news programmes (= that reports are unfair and show favour to one political party)

words.definitionEn: done completely; with great attention to detail
words.exampleEn: a thorough knowledge of the subject

words.definitionEn: very much; completely
words.exampleEn: We thoroughly enjoyed ourselves.

words.definitionEn: a statement in which you tell somebody that you will punish or harm them, especially if they do not do what you want
words.exampleEn: threat against somebody/something, to make threats against somebody

words.definitionEn: to say that you will cause trouble, hurt somebody, etc. if you do not get what you want
words.exampleEn: threaten somebody/something, They broke my windows and threatened me.

words.definitionEn: an offer by a person or a company to pay a particular amount of money for something
words.exampleEn: bid for something, A German firm launched a takeover bid for the company.

words.definitionEn: the short, thick finger at the side of the hand, slightly apart from the other four
words.exampleEn: She still sucks her thumb when she's worried.

words.definitionEn: in this way; like this
words.exampleEn: Many scholars have argued thus.

words.definitionEn: to offer to pay a particular price for something, especially at an auction
words.exampleEn: bid (something) (for something), I bid £2 000 for the painting.

words.definitionEn: to measure how long it takes for something to happen or for somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: time something, ‘How long does it take to get to Leeds from here?’ ‘I don’t know, I’ve never timed it.’

words.definitionEn: the act of choosing when something happens; a particular point or period of time when something happens or is planned
words.exampleEn: The timing of the decision was a complete surprise.

words.definitionEn: a piece of soft paper, used especially as a handkerchief
words.exampleEn: a box of tissues

words.definitionEn: to give a book, piece of music, etc. a particular name
words.exampleEn: be titled…, Their first album was titled ‘Made in Valmez’.

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring weight, in the UK 2 240 pounds (long ton) and in the US 2 000 pounds (short ton)
words.exampleEn: (informal), What have you got in this bag? It weighs a ton (= is very heavy).

words.definitionEn: the quality of somebody’s voice, especially expressing a particular emotion
words.exampleEn: speaking in hushed/low/clipped/measured, etc. tones

words.definitionEn: a unit for measuring weight, equal to 1 000 kilograms
words.exampleEn: a record grain harvest of 236m tonnes

words.definitionEn: to send somebody a bill for something
words.exampleEn: bill somebody for something, Please bill me for the books.

words.definitionEn: having or causing problems or difficulties
words.exampleEn: a tough childhood

words.definitionEn: a sports competition involving a number of teams or players who take part in different games and must leave the competition if they lose. The competition continues until there is only the winner left.
words.exampleEn: a golf/tennis/soccer/chess tournament

words.definitionEn: to find or discover somebody/something by looking carefully for them/it
words.exampleEn: We finally traced him to an address in Chicago.

words.definitionEn: to find somebody/something by following the marks, signs, information, etc., that they have left behind them
words.exampleEn: track somebody/something, hunters tracking and shooting bears

words.definitionEn: the activity of buying and selling things
words.exampleEn: new laws on Sunday trading (= shops being open on Sundays)

words.definitionEn: a very sad event or situation, especially one that involves death
words.exampleEn: It's a tragedy that she died so young.

words.definitionEn: making you feel very sad, usually because somebody has died or suffered a lot
words.exampleEn: He was killed in a tragic accident at the age of 24.

words.definitionEn: a particular quality in your personality
words.exampleEn: personality traits

words.definitionEn: connected with the science of biology
words.exampleEn: the biological sciences

words.definitionEn: the act of moving somebody/something from one place, group or job to another; an occasion when this happens
words.exampleEn: electronic data transfer

words.definitionEn: to move from one place to another; to move something/somebody from one place to another
words.exampleEn: transfer (to something), The film studio is transferring to Hollywood.

words.definitionEn: to change the form of something; to change in form
words.exampleEn: transform something/somebody (into something), The photochemical reactions transform the light into electrical impulses.

words.definitionEn: the process or a period of changing from one state or condition to another
words.exampleEn: transition from something to something, the transition from school to full-time work

words.definitionEn: to send an electronic signal, radio or television broadcast, etc.
words.exampleEn: signals transmitted from a satellite

words.definitionEn: a system for carrying people or goods from one place to another using vehicles, roads, etc.
words.exampleEn: public transportation (= the system of buses, trains, etc. provided for people to travel from one place to another)

words.definitionEn: a piece of equipment for catching animals
words.exampleEn: a fox with its leg in a trap

words.definitionEn: to put somebody in a dangerous place that they cannot get out of
words.exampleEn: be trapped, Help! I'm trapped!

words.definitionEn: a collection of valuable things such as gold, silver and jewellery
words.exampleEn: buried treasure
